title: eysenhardtia orthocarpa
intro: Eysenhardtia orthocarpa, also known as the Mexican buckeye, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the pea family. The medicinal properties of the Mexican buckeye include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of the Mexican buckeye typically involve the root and bark, which can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
